name: mutuality_reciprocal_pass
text: Each party may disclose Confidential Information and each party acts as both a Disclosing Party and Receiving Party.

name: mutuality_reciprocal_destruction_obligation_pass
text:
Each party may disclose Confidential Information, and each party acts as both a Disclosing Party and a Receiving Party.

The Receiving Party has a unilateral obligation to destroy or return all copies of Confidential Information on request.

name: mutuality_one_way_fail
text: This one-way NDA requires only the Receiving Party to protect the Company's information.

name: mutuality_title_only_review
label_note: A mutuality title or heading alone is not an operative reciprocal confidentiality obligation.
text:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

The Receiving Party shall protect the Company's Confidential Information and may use it only for the Purpose.

name: mutuality_non_mutual_label_not_positive_signal
label_note: Non-mutual / without-reciprocity wording must not be counted as a positive mutuality signal.
text: This Agreement is not mutual and is without reciprocity. The Receiving Party shall protect the Company's Confidential Information.

name: governing_law_difc_pass
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the DIFC.

name: governing_law_california_fail
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of California.

name: governing_law_conditional_override_review
label_note: Product judgment (not counsel-final): an approved law with an 'unless otherwise agreed' escape hatch is not a fail, but the governing-law position can move later without review, so surface it. Gate as review only.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the parties.

name: governing_law_approved_with_unapproved_choice_carveout_review
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales except that intellectual property disputes shall be governed by the laws of France.

name: governing_law_missing_fail
text: The parties agree to keep all shared information confidential and to use it only for the purpose of the discussions.

name: governing_law_heading_only_review
text: Governing Law

name: governing_law_foreign_incorporated_party_pass
label_note: Foreign-incorporated counterparty recital ("incorporated under the laws of Singapore") must NOT be read as a competing governing law; the operative clause picks an approved law (India).
text:
Acme Pte Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of Singapore, having its registered office at 1 Raffles Place, Singapore (hereinafter referred to as "Company") of the FIRST PARTY.

Aspora Technologies Private Limited, a company incorporated under the laws of India, having its registered office at Bangalore (hereinafter referred to as "Aspora") of the SECOND PARTY.

GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of any dispute amongst the Parties, the dispute shall be finally resolved by courts in Mumbai.

name: governing_law_foreign_incorporated_party_unapproved_still_fails
label_note: Recital neutralization must not mask an actually-unapproved operative governing law (California).
text:
Acme Pte Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of Singapore, having its registered office at 1 Raffles Place, Singapore (hereinafter referred to as "Company") of the FIRST PARTY.

GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of California.

name: governing_law_only_incorporation_recital_missing
label_note: A party-incorporation recital alone is NOT a governing-law clause; with no operative governing-law statement the clause is missing.
text:
Acme Pte Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of Singapore, having its registered office at 1 Raffles Place, Singapore (hereinafter referred to as "Company") of the FIRST PARTY.

The parties agree to keep all shared information confidential and to use it only for the purpose of the discussions.

name: governing_law_ontario_alias_pass
label_note: Approved-law aliases count only when they modify the operative governing-law clause.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by Ontario laws.

name: governing_law_approved_secondary_unapproved_review
label_note: A secondary unapproved carve-out is not a clean approved-law pass; it needs human review to decide which law controls that topic.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India, provided that enforcement of equitable remedies shall be governed by the laws of Singapore.

name: governing_law_approved_conditional_choice_review
label_note: An approved law subject to a later agreement is not a fail, but it is not a stable governing-law pass.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Delaware unless otherwise agreed by the parties.

name: term_three_years_pass
text: The confidentiality obligations survive for three (3) years.

name: term_five_years_boundary_pass
text: The confidentiality obligations survive for five (5) years after disclosure.

name: term_seven_years_fail
text: The confidentiality obligations survive for seven (7) years.

name: term_perpetual_survival_fail
text: The confidentiality obligations survive indefinitely and in perpetuity.

name: term_survival_decoy_duration_trap
text: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years. The confidentiality obligations survive for seven (7) years after disclosure.

name: term_survival_unresolved_by_reference_trap
text: The confidentiality obligations survive in accordance with Section 7.2 of this Agreement.

name: confidential_information_broad_with_standard_exclusions_pass
text:
Confidential Information means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party.

Confidential Information does not include information that is public, already known, received from a lawful third party, or independently developed without use of the Confidential Information.

name: confidential_information_affirmatively_poisoned_definition_fail
text: Confidential Information means all business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party, and includes information that is publicly available, already known to the Receiving Party, or independently developed by the Receiving Party.

name: confidential_information_unqualified_independent_development_review
label_note: Unqualified short-form independent-development exclusion is near-universal NDA language; product decision to review rather than auto-fail (counsel may upgrade).
text:
Confidential Information means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party.

Confidential Information does not include information that is independently developed by the Receiving Party.

name: confidential_information_reverse_engineering_exclusion_trap
text:
Confidential Information means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party.

Confidential Information does not include information obtained by reverse engineering of disclosed materials.

name: confidential_information_residual_knowledge_exclusion_trap
text:
Confidential Information means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party.

Confidential Information excludes residual knowledge retained in unaided memory.

name: confidential_information_independent_dev_no_access_qualified_pass
text: Confidential Information means all non-public business, technical, and financial information disclosed by either party, whether orally or in writing. Confidential Information does not include information that was independently developed by personnel who had no access to the Confidential Information.

name: confidential_information_usage_right_outside_exclusion_review
label_note: Separate usage-right language can create an extra independent-development carve-out even when the definition itself is broad.
text:
Confidential Information means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party.

The Receiving Party may freely use anything it independently develops.

name: confidential_information_negated_reverse_engineering_not_carveout_pass
label_note: A prohibition on reverse engineering is not a reverse-engineering exclusion or usage right.
text:
Confidential Information means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, market, proprietary and trade secret information disclosed by either party.

The Receiving Party may not reverse engineer Confidential Information or disclosed materials.

name: ai_agreement_confirms_pass_no_escalation
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the DIFC.

ai.reason: The cited paragraph names an approved jurisdiction.

name: ai_disagreement_escalates_to_review
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the DIFC.

ai.reason: Scripted dissent: the governing-law provision is deficient.

name: ai_invalid_citation_escalates_to_review
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the DIFC.

ai.reason: Scripted pass with a fabricated citation.

name: ai_low_confidence_escalates_to_review
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the DIFC.

name: ai_cannot_soften_deterministic_fail_floor
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of California.

ai.reason: Scripted AI calls the failing clause acceptable.

name: verifier_affirms_carveout_pass_without_over_escalating
label_note: A genuine freedom-to-deal carve-out the engine correctly clears must survive the always-on prohibited-pass verification: the adversary affirms the clear instead of over-escalating it to review. (The real default_verifier affirms a carve-out pass; a hallucinated clear with a cited restriction is what its refute path catches.)
text: Each party shall not be restricted from dealing with any contact introduced by the other party.

verifier.rationale: Freedom-to-deal carve-out correctly cleared; there is no restriction to refute.

name: verifier_refutes_false_flagged_carveout_fail_to_review
label_note: The engine false-flags a genuine freedom-to-deal carve-out as a restriction. Under the fail-open verifier policy, a refutation no longer auto-clears to pass from this state; it must route to human review rather than false-clearing.
text: Neither party shall be prevented from dealing directly with introduced parties.

verifier.rationale: Freedom-to-deal carve-out, not a restriction; the engine inverted the polarity.

name: verifier_affirm_keeps_genuine_restriction_failed
label_note: An affirming verifier must leave a genuine restriction failed (no over-correction).
text: The Recipient must not circumvent the Company or deal directly with introduced parties.

verifier.rationale: Genuine non-circumvention restriction; the engine finding stands.

name: non_circumvention_freedom_preserving_carveout_pass
label_note: Freedom-preserving carve-out: literal no-restriction wording should pass rather than keyword-match as a prohibited restraint.
text: Nothing in this Agreement restricts either party from dealing with third parties or contacts introduced by the other party.

verifier.rationale: The text preserves freedom to deal and does not impose a non-circumvention restriction.

name: non_circumvention_negated_disclaimer_pass
label_note: Negated reference/disclaimer: mentioning non-circumvention only to say no such obligation is created must not fail.
text: For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement does not create any non-circumvention, exclusive dealing, or non-solicitation obligation.

verifier.rationale: This is a negated disclaimer, not an operative business restraint.

name: non_circumvention_unlisted_hiring_verb_not_pass
label_note: Unlisted verbs can still be a prohibited introduced-party restraint; hire/recruit language must never be silently passed.
text: Recipient shall not hire, recruit, or retain any introduced party, employee, consultant, or adviser introduced by Discloser in connection with the Transaction.

verifier.rationale: The clause imposes an introduced-party hiring and recruiting restriction beyond confidentiality.

name: non_circumvention_ambiguous_present_language_reviews
label_note: Present non-circumvention language with uncertain operative effect should route to review rather than pass or fail.
text: The parties acknowledge non-circumvention and exclusive dealing principles for any introduced opportunity, subject to terms to be agreed in a future definitive agreement.

verifier.rationale: The language signals non-circumvention but does not clearly state the operative restriction or scope.

name: non_circumvention_lawful_circumvention_only_not_fail
label_note: Circumvention of laws/regulatory obligations is not the prohibited commercial non-circumvention concept; even if keyword matching is uncertain, it must not remain a non-circumvention fail.
text: No party may circumvent applicable laws, sanctions, or regulatory obligations when handling Confidential Information.

verifier.rationale: This is only about legal compliance and does not restrict dealings with introduced parties or commercial opportunities.

name: term_non_confidential_duration_decoys_pass
label_note: Long audit/tax/payment/records periods are decoys and should not be counted as ordinary confidentiality survival when confidentiality is capped.
text: The parties shall retain audit, tax, payment, warranty, insurance, employment, and books and records files for seven (7) years. The confidentiality obligations survive for five (5) years after termination.

name: term_trade_secret_legal_carveout_long_pass
label_note: Long or indefinite survival is acceptable when scoped to trade secrets or legal/regulatory obligations, while ordinary confidentiality is capped.
text: The confidentiality obligations survive for five (5) years, except that trade secrets and information subject to legal or regulatory obligations remain protected for as long as such protected status or law requires.

name: term_ordinary_confidentiality_seven_years_fail
label_note: The same seven-year duration fails when tied to ordinary confidentiality rather than a narrow trade-secret/legal carve-out.
text: All ordinary confidentiality obligations survive for seven (7) years after termination.

name: non_circumvention_non_compete_not_pass
label_note: Wave 2 prohibited-position family: non-compete language must not be silently passed by AI-only review.
text: The Recipient shall not compete with the Discloser in any business using the disclosed opportunity for two years.

verifier.rationale: This is a prohibited non-compete restriction, not an acceptable NDA confidentiality term.

name: non_circumvention_ip_assignment_not_pass
label_note: Wave 2 prohibited-position family: broad IP assignment language belongs outside this NDA and must not silently pass.
text: Recipient hereby assigns all right, title and interest in intellectual property developed from the Confidential Information to Discloser.

verifier.rationale: This is a prohibited IP assignment position in an NDA.

name: non_circumvention_liquidated_damages_not_pass
label_note: Wave 2 prohibited-position family: penalty/liquidated/punitive damages language should not be silently accepted.
text: Any breach of this Agreement will require the Recipient to pay liquidated damages of $500,000 as a penalty, without proof of loss.

verifier.rationale: This is a prohibited liquidated-damages or penalty position.

name: non_circumvention_auto_renew_lock_not_pass
label_note: Wave 2 prohibited-position family: evergreen/auto-renew/no-termination lock should not silently pass.
text: This Agreement automatically renews for successive one-year terms and the Recipient may not terminate while discussions continue.

verifier.rationale: This is a prohibited auto-renewal and no-termination lock.

name: governing_law_ai_first_real_california_fail
label_note: Real-AI gate (skipped when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is absent): the primary AI on the live AI-first path must FAIL a clearly-named unapproved jurisdiction (California) on its own. The deterministic governing-law backstop was removed once the model proved reliable here, so this case is the honest proof that the AI -- not a backstop -- catches an unapproved operative law.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of California.

name: governing_law_ai_first_real_england_pass
label_note: Real-AI gate (skipped when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is absent): an approved jurisdiction (England and Wales) must PASS on the live AI-first path -- the removed backstop must not have left the engine over-failing approved law.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales.

name: governing_law_ai_first_real_incorporation_recital_not_failed
label_note: Real-AI gate (skipped when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is absent): an incorporation recital naming California ('incorporated under the laws of California') must NOT make governing_law FAIL when the operative clause names England and Wales. Confirms the recital-precision the removed backstop guarded is now upheld by the AI itself.
text:
Acme Inc, a company incorporated under the laws of California, having its registered office at 1 Market Street, San Francisco (hereinafter "Company") of the FIRST PARTY.

GOVERNING LAW: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.

name: governing_law_approved_india_pass
label_note: An approved jurisdiction (India) passes on the deterministic path -- baseline approved-law coverage.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.

name: governing_law_ai_first_conditional_review
label_note: A conditional escape clause ('unless otherwise agreed') on an approved jurisdiction is unclear, not a clear unapproved fail -- the case must remain review on the AI-first verifier path.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the parties.

verifier.rationale: Approved governing law with conditional escape; surfaces as review.

name: cat_a_case1_multiprong_restraint_fail
label_note: Category-A /tmp/catA/case1 (hc01 multiprong restraint). Section 6 stacks four prohibited prongs (auto-renew lock, perpetual survival, introduced-party non-solicit/no-hire, USD 250k penalty) in one paragraph; ground truth = FAIL. The AI-first stub fails the present introduced-party non-solicit restraint and the affirm verifier keeps the fail.
text:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AND INFORMATION PROTECTION AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure and Information Protection Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of 4 February 2026 (the "Effective Date") by and between Brightwater Logistics Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of England and Wales with registered number 08812233 and registered office at 14 Harbour Exchange Square, London E14 9GE, United Kingdom ("Brightwater"), and Aspora Technologies Inc., a company incorporated under the laws of the State of Delaware with offices at 2261 Market Street, San Francisco, California 94114, United States ("Aspora"). Each is a "Party" and together the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to explore a potential business relationship relating to cross-border logistics orchestration and embedded payment settlement (the "Purpose");

WHEREAS, in connection with the Purpose each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential and proprietary information; and

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to set out the terms on which such information will be protected.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and undertakings set out below, and for other good and valuable consideration the receipt and sufficiency of which are acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows.

1. Mutual Obligations

1.1 This is a mutual, two-way Agreement. Each Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party, and each Party acts as both a Disclosing Party and a Receiving Party. The confidentiality and non-use obligations apply equally and reciprocally to each Party in its capacity as a Receiving Party.

1.2 Each Receiving Party shall (a) hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) use it solely for the Purpose; (c) protect it using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information of like importance, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; and (d) limit access to those of its representatives who have a need to know it for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those set out herein.

1.3 Each Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its representatives.

2. Confidential Information

2.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, employee, supplier, pricing, market, product, proprietary, trade secret and source code information disclosed by either Party, including the right of publicity and the existence and terms of this Agreement, whether or not marked confidential.

2.2 Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes public through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was already in the receiving Party's possession without obligation of confidentiality; (c) is rightfully received from a third party without obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed by the receiving Party without any use of, access to, or reference to the disclosing Party's Confidential Information.

2.3 Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to disclose any particular information or to proceed with any transaction.

3. Permitted Disclosures

3.1 A Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or valid legal process, provided that (where lawful and practicable) it gives the Disclosing Party prompt prior written notice and reasonable cooperation so the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.

3.2 Any such compelled disclosure shall be limited to that portion of the Confidential Information that the Receiving Party is legally required to disclose.

4. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

4.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales, without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles.

4.2 The Parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales in respect of any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

5. Data Protection

5.1 To the extent either Party processes personal data of the other in connection with the Purpose, it shall do so in compliance with applicable data protection laws, including the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018, and shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect such personal data.

6. Protection of the Relationship and Remedies

This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall automatically renew for successive twelve (12) month terms, and neither Party shall have any right to terminate this Agreement for so long as the other Party continues to hold any Confidential Information. The confidentiality and non-use obligations of each Party shall survive in perpetuity and shall remain binding indefinitely. During the term and for twenty-four (24) months thereafter, neither Party shall, directly or indirectly, solicit, hire, or engage any employee, consultant, or contractor of the other Party who was introduced or became known to it in connection with this Agreement. In the event of any breach of this Section, the breaching Party shall pay to the other Party liquidated damages of USD 250,000 per breach as a penalty, without the need to prove actual loss.

7. Return or Destruction of Materials

7.1 Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, or upon expiry or termination of the discussions relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information in its possession or control and, if requested, certify such return or destruction in writing.

7.2 The Receiving Party may retain one archival copy solely to the extent required by applicable law or its bona fide internal document-retention policy, which copy shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.

8. No Warranty

8.1 All Confidential Information is provided "as is." Neither Party makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of its Confidential Information.

9. No Licence

9.1 Except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose, no licence or other right under any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret, or other intellectual property right is granted or implied by this Agreement.

10. General

10.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties concerning its subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings.

10.2 Amendment. This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument signed by both Parties.

10.3 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the other Party's prior written consent, except to a successor in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all of its assets.

10.4 Severability. If any provision of this Agreement is held unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

10.5 Waiver. No waiver of any provision shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving Party.

10.6 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, including by electronic signature, each of which shall be deemed an original.

11. Execution

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first written above.

For and on behalf of Brightwater Logistics Ltd

By: _______________________________

Name: Marcus T. Delacroix

Title: Managing Director

Date: 4 February 2026

For and on behalf of Aspora Technologies Inc.

By: _______________________________

Name: Priya N. Raghavan

Title: Chief Operating Officer

Date: 4 February 2026

verifier.rationale: The introduced-party non-solicit/no-hire restraint is genuinely present; affirm the failed finding.

name: cat_a_case2_crossclause_survival_fail
label_note: Category-A /tmp/catA/case2 (hc05 cross-clause survival). The §2 CI-definition rider ('for so long as the information retains any commercial value') plus the §11 deferral back to it make ordinary CI survival perpetual; ground truth = term_and_survival FAIL. The deterministic engine reads the perpetual/value-based rider over the whole text and fails indefinite survival.
text:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of 28 January 2026 (the "Effective Date") by and between Vantage Freight Holdings Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of England and Wales with registered number 07733410 and registered office at 1 Kingsway, London WC2B 6AN, United Kingdom ("Vantage"), and Aspora Technologies Inc., a company incorporated under the laws of the State of Delaware with offices at 2261 Market Street, San Francisco, California 94114, United States ("Aspora"). Each is a "Party" and together the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to evaluate a potential commercial relationship relating to freight-finance settlement and treasury services (the "Purpose");

WHEREAS, in connection with the Purpose each Party may disclose confidential and proprietary information to the other; and

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to protect such information on the terms set out below.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants set out below, the Parties agree as follows.

1. Mutual Obligations

1.1 This is a mutual, two-way Agreement. Each Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party, and each Party acts as both a Disclosing Party and a Receiving Party. The confidentiality and non-use obligations apply equally and reciprocally to each Party as a Receiving Party.

1.2 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence, use it solely for the Purpose, and disclose it only to representatives with a need to know who are bound by obligations no less protective than those herein.

2. Confidential Information

2.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, employee, supplier, pricing, market, product, proprietary, trade secret and source code information disclosed by either Party, including the right of publicity and the existence and terms of this Agreement, whether or not marked confidential. Each Party's obligations of confidentiality and non-use with respect to such Confidential Information shall continue, and such information shall remain Confidential Information, for so long as the information retains any commercial value to the disclosing Party.

2.2 Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes public through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was already in the receiving Party's possession without obligation of confidentiality; (c) is rightfully received from a third party without obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed by the receiving Party without any use of, access to, or reference to the disclosing Party's Confidential Information.

3. Use and Protection

3.1 Each Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and shall protect it using at least a reasonable degree of care, and in no event less than the degree of care it applies to its own confidential information of like importance.

3.2 Each Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party upon becoming aware of any unauthorised use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information and shall reasonably cooperate to mitigate the effects of such unauthorised use or disclosure.

4. Permitted and Compelled Disclosures

4.1 A Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or valid legal or regulatory process, provided that, where lawful and practicable, it gives the Disclosing Party prompt prior written notice and reasonable cooperation to enable the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.

4.2 Disclosure compelled under Section 4.1 shall not, of itself, cause the disclosed information to lose its status as Confidential Information for any other purpose.

5. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

5.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.

5.2 The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

6. No Restriction on Business Dealings

6.1 Nothing in this Agreement restricts either Party from dealing with any third party, and this Agreement creates no non-circumvention, non-competition, or non-solicitation obligation of any kind.

7. Data Protection

7.1 Each Party shall comply with applicable data protection laws in respect of any personal data processed in connection with the Purpose and shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures.

8. Intellectual Property

8.1 All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. Except for the limited right to use it for the Purpose, no licence or other right under any intellectual property right is granted or implied.

9. No Warranty

9.1 All Confidential Information is provided "as is," and neither Party warrants its accuracy or completeness.

10. Return or Destruction

10.1 Upon the Disclosing Party's written request, or on expiry or termination, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, subject to a single archival copy retained as required by law or bona fide retention policy.

11. Term and Survival

11.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a fixed term of three (3) years, unless earlier terminated by either Party on thirty (30) days' written notice. Following expiry or termination, the confidentiality and non-use obligations of each Party shall survive for the period during which the relevant information remains Confidential Information as defined in Section 2.

12. General

12.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties on its subject matter and supersedes all prior understandings.

12.2 Amendment; Waiver. Any amendment or waiver must be in writing and signed by both Parties.

12.3 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the other's prior written consent, save to a successor in a merger or sale of substantially all assets.

12.4 Severability. If any provision is held unenforceable, the remainder shall continue in effect.

12.5 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, including by electronic signature.

13. Execution

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first written above.

For and on behalf of Vantage Freight Holdings Ltd

By: _______________________________

Name: Daniel R. Forsythe

Title: Director

Date: 28 January 2026

For and on behalf of Aspora Technologies Inc.

By: _______________________________

Name: Meera T. Iyer

Title: Chief Operating Officer

Date: 28 January 2026

name: cat_a_case3_law_forum_split_governing_law_flag
label_note: Category-A /tmp/catA/case3 (x1 govlaw/venue split). §7 governing law = England & Wales (approved) but §8 exclusive forum = courts of the Cayman Islands (unapproved); ground truth = the law/forum split must be flagged, not pass clean. The deterministic engine surfaces the unapproved Cayman forum and escalates governing_law to review.
text:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of 12 March 2026 (the "Effective Date") by and between Acme Corp, a company incorporated under the laws of England and Wales with registered number 06622019 and registered office at 30 Crown Place, London EC2A 4EB, United Kingdom ("Acme"), and Aspora Technologies Limited, a company incorporated under the laws of England and Wales with offices at 70 St Mary Axe, London EC3A 8BE, United Kingdom ("Aspora"). Each is a "Party" and together the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to explore a potential business relationship (the "Purpose") and may exchange confidential information in connection with it; and

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to protect that information on the terms below.

NOW, THEREFORE, the Parties agree as follows.

1. Confidential Information

1.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing and proprietary information disclosed by either party, in any form, together with the existence and terms of this Agreement.

1.2 Confidential Information does not include information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Recipient, was lawfully known before disclosure, is rightfully received from a third party without restriction, or is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information.

2. Mutual Obligations

2.1 This is a mutual Agreement. Each party may act as a Discloser and as a Recipient, and the obligations apply reciprocally.

2.2 Each Recipient shall hold the Discloser's Confidential Information in confidence, use it solely to evaluate the potential business relationship, and disclose it only to representatives with a need to know who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those herein.

3. Use and Protection

3.1 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely to evaluate the potential business relationship and shall protect it using at least a reasonable degree of care.

3.2 The Recipient shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its representatives.

4. Permitted Disclosures

4.1 The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process, provided that, where lawful and practicable, it gives the Discloser prompt prior written notice and reasonable cooperation to seek a protective order.

5. Term and Survival

5.1 This Agreement remains in effect for two (2) years, and the confidentiality obligations survive for three (3) years from the date of disclosure.

6. No Restriction on Dealings

6.1 Nothing in this Agreement restricts either party from dealing with any third party, and this Agreement creates no non-circumvention, non-competition, or non-solicitation obligation of any kind.

7. Governing Law

7.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.

8. Jurisdiction and Venue

8.1 The parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Cayman Islands for any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, and waive any objection to proceedings in that forum on the grounds of venue or forum non conveniens.

9. Data Protection

9.1 Each party shall comply with applicable data protection laws in respect of any personal data processed in connection with the Purpose.

10. Intellectual Property

10.1 All Confidential Information remains the property of the Discloser. No licence under any intellectual property right is granted except the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

11. No Warranty

11.1 All Confidential Information is provided "as is," and neither party warrants its accuracy or completeness.

12. Return or Destruction

12.1 Upon the Discloser's written request, or on expiry or termination, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy the Confidential Information, subject to one archival copy retained as required by law.

13. General

13.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties on its subject matter.

13.2 Amendment; Waiver. Any amendment or waiver must be in writing and signed by both parties.

13.3 Severability. If any provision is held unenforceable, the remainder shall continue in effect.

13.4 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, including by electronic signature.

14. Signatures

Signed for and on behalf of Acme Corp

By: _______________________________

Name: ____________________

Title: ____________________

Date: ____________________

Signed for and on behalf of Aspora Technologies Limited

By: _______________________________

Name: ____________________

Title: ____________________

Date: ____________________

name: cat_a_case4_compound_buried_noncompete_flag
label_note: Category-A /tmp/catA/case4 (compound monster: notwithstanding-kills-carveouts + MSA shall-prevail + 18-month non-compete framed as a use restriction in §4.1). Ground truth = FAIL on each defect; the operative non-compete restraint must not pass clean. The AI-first stub misses the buried restraint and clears non_circumvention; the adversarial verifier refutes the hallucinated clear, downgrading it to review.
text:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of 19 February 2026 (the "Effective Date") by and between Initech LLC, a limited liability company organised under the laws of the State of Delaware with offices at 500 Technology Drive, Wilmington, Delaware 19801, United States ("Initech"), and Aspora Technologies Limited, a company incorporated under the laws of England and Wales with offices at 70 St Mary Axe, London EC3A 8BE, United Kingdom ("Aspora"). Each is a "Party" and together the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, the Parties have an existing Master Services Agreement and wish to exchange confidential information in connection with a potential expansion of their relationship (the "Purpose"); and

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to protect such information on the terms set out below.

NOW, THEREFORE, the Parties agree as follows.

1. Confidential Information

1.1 "Confidential Information" means any and all non-public business, financial, technical, customer, supplier, pricing, and proprietary information disclosed by either party, in any form, together with the existence and terms of this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the standard exclusions in Section 2 shall not apply where the Discloser notifies the Recipient that it considers the information sensitive.

2. Exclusions

2.1 Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Recipient; (b) was already lawfully known to the Recipient without obligation of confidentiality; (c) is rightfully received from a third party without obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed by the Recipient without use of the Confidential Information.

3. Obligations and Relationship to Other Agreements

3.1 The Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in confidence, use it solely to evaluate the relationship, and protect it with at least reasonable care.

3.2 All obligations of the parties under this Agreement are subject to, and shall be construed in accordance with, the terms of the Master Services Agreement between the parties dated 1 January 2024, the terms of which are incorporated herein by reference and shall prevail in the event of any conflict.

4. Use Restriction

4.1 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely to evaluate and pursue the Purpose. The Recipient shall, during the term of this Agreement and for a period of eighteen (18) months following its termination, not use any Confidential Information, nor any knowledge or relationships derived from it, to participate in, advise, or invest in any business that is engaged in activities competitive with the Discloser in the field to which the Confidential Information relates.

5. Permitted Disclosures

5.1 The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process, provided that, where lawful and practicable, it gives the Discloser prompt prior written notice.

6. Term and Survival

6.1 This Agreement remains in effect for two (2) years, and the confidentiality obligations survive for two (2) years from the date of disclosure.

7. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

7.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Delaware.

7.2 The parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the State of Delaware.

8. Data Protection

8.1 Each party shall comply with applicable data protection laws in respect of any personal data processed in connection with the Purpose.

9. Intellectual Property

9.1 All Confidential Information remains the property of the Discloser. No licence under any intellectual property right is granted except the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

10. No Warranty

10.1 All Confidential Information is provided "as is," and neither party warrants its accuracy or completeness.

11. Return or Destruction

11.1 Upon the Discloser's written request, or on expiry or termination, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy the Confidential Information, subject to one archival copy retained as required by law.

12. General

12.1 Entire Agreement. Subject to Section 3.2, this Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties on the subject matter of confidentiality.

12.2 Amendment; Waiver. Any amendment or waiver must be in writing and signed by both parties.

12.3 Severability. If any provision is held unenforceable, the remainder shall continue in effect.

12.4 Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, including by electronic signature.

13. Signatures

Signed for and on behalf of Initech LLC

By: _______________________________

Name: ____________________

Title: ____________________

Date: ____________________

Signed for and on behalf of Aspora Technologies Limited

By: _______________________________

Name: ____________________

Title: ____________________

Date: ____________________

verifier.rationale: The §4.1 18-month non-compete framed as a use restriction is a prohibited operative restraint the clear missed; refute the pass.

name: cat_a_case5_real_nda_perpetual_survival_fail
label_note: Category-A /tmp/catA/case5 (real slice-bank NDA + injected defects). The NATIVE defect already in the real document is perpetual ordinary survival ('will survive ... for perpetuity', unscoped, applied to all rights/obligations); ground truth = should be capped/scoped. The deterministic engine catches the unscoped perpetual survival and fails indefinite survival on the genuine long NDA.
text:
NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is executed on ___________ (“Execution Date”) at Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

By and between:

slice small finance bank limited (erstwhile North East Small Finance Bank Limited), a small finance bank incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, and having a license to operate as a bank in India under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, having its registered office at G.S. Point, G.S. Road, Vill- Sarumataria, Opp. Dispur Fire Station, Sachivalaya, Dispur, Guwahati, 781006, Assam, India, and also having its office at Indiqube Ashford Park View, Municipal No.9, 80 Feet Rd, Industrial Layout, Koramangala 3rd block, Ward No 68, Bengaluru - 560034, Karnataka, herein referred to as “Bank”, which expression shall unless repugnant to the context or the meaning thereof include its successors and permitted assigns;

AND

_______________, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, and having its registered office at __________, hereinafter referred to as "Company", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the subject or context, be deemed to include its successors and permitted assigns.

The Bank and Company are individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS:

Parties are desirous of entering into discussions and negotiations for a possible business relationship relating to ___________, referred to as the “Purpose”.

The Bank and the Company shall be sharing certain information including Confidential Information (defined below) with respect to the Purpose.

The Bank and the Company desire to protect the confidentiality of, maintain their respective rights in, and prevent the unauthorized use and disclosure of such Confidential Information.

As an express condition to the disclosure of Confidential Information, the Parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement out of their own free will and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions herein after setforth, governing the disclosure, use and protection of the Confidential Information.

NOW THEREFORE IN CONSIDERATION OF THE PREMISES AND MUTUAL OBLIGATIONS HEREINAFTER DESCRIBED THE RECEIPT AND SUFFICIENCY OF WHICH IS HEREBY ACKNOWLEDGED, THE PARTIES HEREBY COVENANT AND AGREE AS FOLLOWS:

Definitions

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires:

“Disclosing Party” shall mean the party disclosing Confidential Information (defined below).

“Recipient”/ “Receiving Party” shall mean the party receiving Confidential Information (defined below).

“Intellectual Property” shall mean the patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade-secrets, records and logs pertaining to the business and / or the activities of a Party.

Interpretation

In this Agreement, unless the subject or context otherwise requires:

References to the singular shall include the plural and vice versa.

References to Clause shall be references to Clauses of this Agreement.

The term “include”, “including” and grammatical variations thereof shall be construed without limitation.

If any word or phrase is defined, its other grammatical forms have a corresponding meaning.

The terms “hereof”, “hereto” and derivative or similar words refer to this entire Agreement or specified Clauses of this Agreement, as the context may require.

All recitals, schedule and annexure hereto shall constitute an integral part of this Agreement.

A reference to a statute or statutory provision includes, to the extent applicable at any relevant time, that statute or statutory provision as from time to time amended, consolidated, modified, re-enacted or replaced by any other statute or statutory provision and any subordinate legislation or regulation made under the relevant statute or statutory provision.

Clause headings and titles used are for ease of reference only and in no way define, limit, extend or describe the scope of this Agreement or any provisions hereof.

Words denoting one gender shall include all genders.

Any reference to any agreement or document shall be construed as an amended, varied, supplemented or novated in writing at the relevant time in accordance with the requirements of such agreement or document and if applicable to this Agreement with respect to amendments.

Any approval, permission, consent, acceptance or waiver required from any of the Parties shall mean a requirement for such approval, permission, consent, acceptance or waiver in writing.

A reference to writing includes a facsimile transmission and any means of reproducing words in a tangible and permanently visible form.

Reference to a “month” or a “year” shall be to a calendar month or calendar year, respectively.

Confidentiality Obligations

“Confidential Information” means any data or information that is proprietary to the Disclosing Party and not generally known to the public, whether in tangible or intangible form, in whatever medium provided, whether unmodified or modified by Receiving Party or its Representatives, including but not limited to:

any material discussions / information shared by the Bank pertaining to API documentation, technical specifications, system architecture, and any related operational or security information.

any and/or all information which is commercially sensitive or of a secret nature including data shared by the Bank, including Personally Identifiable Information (“PII”), if any.

plans for products or services, and customer or supplier lists;

any scientific information, invention, design, process, procedure, formula, improvement, technology or method;

Confidential Information need not be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitute a trade secret in order to be designated Confidential Information.  The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Confidential Information is proprietary to the Disclosing Party, has been developed and obtained through great efforts by the Disclosing Party and that Disclosing Party regards all of its Confidential Information as trade secrets.

such Party's sales materials, marketing materials, advertising materials and developments, reports, including and not limited to all quality management methods, quotations, plans, pricing methods, customers’ materials, service pricing, future service lines, marketing strategies, reporting and advertising campaigns, etc. owned/developed by such Party;

Any other information that such Party claims as confidential.

Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, none of the exclusions set out below shall apply, and information shall remain Confidential Information in all cases, where the Disclosing Party has notified the Receiving Party in writing that it regards the information as sensitive.

Notwithstanding anything in the foregoing to the contrary, the term, “Confidential Information” shall not include information which:

was lawfully possessed, as evidenced by the Receiving Party’s records, by the Receiving Party prior to receiving the Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party;

becomes rightfully known by the Receiving Party from a third-party source not under an obligation to Disclosing Party to maintain confidentiality;

is generally known by the public through no fault of or failure to act by the Receiving Party inconsistent with its obligations under this Agreement;

is required to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative proceeding or is otherwise requested or required to be disclosed by law or regulation; and (e) is or has been independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without violation of the terms of this Agreement, as evidenced by the Receiving Party’s records, and without reference or access to any Confidential Information.

Disclosure of Confidential Information:

The Confidential Information shall be used only during the Term (as defined below in this Agreement) and only for the Purpose as defined in the Agreement. From time to time, the Disclosing Party may disclose Confidential Information to the Receiving Party.

The Receiving Party will:

limit disclosure and dissemination of any Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to any third party, except, where applicable, to only to those partners, directors, associates, officers, employees, agents, professional advisors, financial sources or representatives (collectively “Representatives”) who have a need to know such Confidential Information in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties to which this Agreement relates, and only for that purpose;

advise its Representatives of the proprietary nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement, require such Representatives to be bound by written confidentiality restrictions no less stringent than those contained herein, and assume full liability for acts or omissions by its Representatives that are inconsistent with its obligations under this Agreement;

keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential by using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than the degree of care used by it in safeguarding its own confidential information; and

not disclose any Confidential Information received by it to any third parties (except as otherwise provided for herein).

take adequate measures to ensure that there is no co-mingling of Disclosing Party’s Data/ Confidential Information and any other information /documents, records, assets etc. that belong to the Receiving Party or its other customers.

Use of Confidential Information:

The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the Parties and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party.  No other right or license, whether expressed or implied, in the Confidential Information is granted to the Receiving Party hereunder. Title to the Confidential Information will remain solely in the Disclosing Party.  All use of Confidential Information by the Receiving Party shall be for the benefit of the Disclosing Party and any modifications and improvements thereof by the Receiving Party shall be the sole property of the Disclosing Party.

The Receiving Party and its representatives undertake that they will take all reasonable measures to ensure and protect the privacy of personal information of the other Party.

The Receiving Party shall maintain and store Disclosing Party’s information and Data in India.

During the Term and for a period of eighteen (18) months following its expiry or termination, the Receiving Party shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit, hire, or engage any employee, consultant, contractor, customer, or supplier of the Disclosing Party who was introduced to or became known to the Receiving Party in connection with the Purpose. In the event of any breach of this provision, the Receiving Party shall pay to the Disclosing Party liquidated damages of INR 20,000,000 per breach as a penalty, without the need to prove actual loss.

Compelled disclosure of Confidential Information:

Notwithstanding anything in the foregoing to the contrary, the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information pursuant to any governmental, judicial, or administrative order, discovery request, regulatory request or similar method, provided that the Receiving Party promptly notifies, to the extent practicable, the Disclosing Party in writing of such demand for disclosure so that the Disclosing Party, at its sole expense, may seek to make such disclosure subject to a protective order or other appropriate remedy to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information; provided that the Receiving Party will disclose only that portion of the requested Confidential Information that, in the written opinion of its legal counsel, it is required to disclose. The Receiving Party agrees that it shall not oppose and shall cooperate with efforts by, to the extent practicable, the Disclosing Party with respect to any such request for a protective order or other relief. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if the Disclosing Party is unable to obtain or does not seek a protective order and the Receiving Party is legally requested or required to disclose such Confidential Information, disclosure of such Confidential Information may be made without liability.

In consideration of Disclosing Party agreeing to disclose Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees and undertakes:

To hold the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence and secret and treat the Confidential Information with the same degree of care, secrecy and security measures which it affords to its own confidential information;

To use the Confidential Information solely in connection with and as necessary for the Purpose which have been approved in writing by the Disclosing Party;

Not to share, release, disclose or make available the Confidential Information to any other person, (other than the Representatives who need to be aware of the Confidential Information for the Purpose which have been approved by Disclosing Party in writing);

To ensure that its Representatives to whom Confidential Information is required to be disclosed to or shared with, keep such information confidential and do not do anything which would constitute a breach of this Agreement;

To take all prudent steps to prevent unintentional use or disclosure (including by its officers, employees, contractors and Representatives) of the Confidential Information to any other person or company;

Not to copy or make copies of the Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's prior consent and mark all such copies as confidential;

To immediately notify the Disclosing Party of any suspected unauthorised use, copying or disclosure of Confidential Information;

To keep the Confidential Information and any copies thereof secure and in such a way so as to prevent unauthorized access or use by any third party;

To provide assistance reasonably requested by the Disclosing Party in relation to any proceedings the Disclosing Party may take against any person for unauthorised access, copying or disclosure of the Confidential Information;

To deliver or erase or destroy all Confidential Information (including copies thereof) which has come into its possession, immediately if the Disclosing Party so directs in writing; regardless of how the Confidential Information is embodied at the date of the request, save and except the Confidential information the Recipient is required to retain as per applicable law; and

Not make any disclosure or announcement concerning, or otherwise publicise, the details and/or status of any discussions or negotiations that may take place between the Receiving Party or its Representatives and the Disclosing Party, or any other arrangement with the Disclosing Party.

Ownership of Confidential Information:

The Receiving Party agrees that the Disclosing Party is and shall remain the exclusive owner of the Confidential Information and all patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or service marks or logos (whether registered or unregistered and with or without goodwill) and other intellectual property rights, title to or interests therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Receiving Party is granted or implied under this Agreement.

The Disclosing Party may at any time, at its sole discretion, require the Receiving Party to discontinue the usage of any or all Confidential Information and shall notify the Receiving Party of the same and the Receiving Party shall discontinue the usage of any or all Confidential Information forthwith.

Within seven (7) days of delivery of such notification to the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party shall either promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party and destroy all written Confidential Information as directed by the Disclosing Party and any other materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the delivery or destruction, as applicable, of all such materials in writing to the Disclosing Party, save and except the Confidential information the Recipient is required to retain as per applicable law.

If the Disclosing Party directs the destruction of the Confidential Information the Receiving Party shall permanently destroy and remove all Confidential Information including from any computer, disk or other device containing Confidential Information and give in writing an undertaking to the effect that all the Confidential Information has been removed or destroyed, save and except the Confidential information the Recipient is required to retain as per applicable law.

No Representation or Warranty

The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of the Disclosing Party's Affiliates, representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, and neither the Disclosing Party nor any of the Disclosing Party's Affiliates, representatives or agents will have any liability to the Receiving Party or any other person resulting from the use of or reliance upon the Confidential Information. Furthermore, nothing contained herein shall constitute an obligation on the part of the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates or any of their representatives or agents to update any Confidential Information provided hereunder.

Parties shall not reverse-engineer, decompile, or disassemble any equipment, hardware or software provided or disclosed to it and shall not remove, overprint or deface any notice of copyright, trademark, logo, legend or other notice of ownership from any originals or copies of Confidential Information it obtains from the Disclosing Party.

Return of Confidential Information

Receiving Party shall immediately within 7 (seven) days return and redeliver to Disclosing Party all tangible material embodying any Confidential Information provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of the foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data, word processing, or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Confidential Information, in whatever form of storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Disclosing Party may so request; provided however that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies and applicable laws.  Alternatively, the Receiving Party, with the written consent of the Disclosing Party may (or in the case of Notes, at the Receiving Party’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Confidential Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party supervising the destruction).

Notice of Breach

Receiving Party shall within 24 (twenty-four) hours notify the Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of, or suspicion of, (1) any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information by Receiving Party or its Representatives; or (2) any actions by Receiving Party or its Representatives inconsistent with their respective obligations under this Agreement, Receiving Party shall cooperate with any and all efforts of the Disclosing Party to help the Disclosing Party regain possession of Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use.

No Binding Agreement for Transaction

The parties agree that neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement, except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.  The parties further acknowledge and agree that they each reserve the right, in their sole and absolute discretion, to reject any and all proposals and to terminate discussions and negotiations with respect to a Transaction at any time.  This Agreement does not create a joint venture or partnership between the parties.  If a Transaction goes forward, the non-disclosure provisions of any applicable transaction documents entered into between the parties (or their respective affiliates) for the Transaction shall supersede this Agreement. In the event such provision is not provided for in said transaction documents, this Agreement shall control.

Term, Termination and Survival of Obligations

This Agreement shall be valid for a period of three (3) months commencing from the Execution Date unless a definitive agreement is executed between the Parties, in which event this Agreement shall expire on the execution of such definitive agreement (“Term”) and the confidentiality provisions of the definitive agreement shall supersede this Agreement. This Agreement can be terminated by Bank by giving a prior written notice of 7 (seven) days. The rights and obligations of the Parties mentioned in this Agreement will survive the expiry or early termination of this Agreement for perpetuity after the termination of this Agreement.

This Agreement shall stand terminated on the date upon which the Parties enter into a final definitive agreement, with each other, on mutually accepted terms, in relation to the Transaction.

Indemnification

The Receiving Party ("Indemnifying Party") agrees to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Disclosing Party, its officers, directors, employees, agents, and representatives (collectively, the "Indemnified Party") from and against any and all claims, demands, actions, suits, losses, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or related to:

Any breach or violation of this Agreement by the Indemnifying Party, including any unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information;

Any willful misconduct, negligence, or unlawful act by the Indemnifying Party, its employees, agents, or contractors in connection with this Agreement; or

Any claims by third parties resulting from the Indemnifying Party’s actions or omissions under this Agreement.

Injunctive Relief

Receiving Party acknowledges that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement could cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or an inadequate remedy.  The Receiving Party therefore agrees that the Disclosing Party will have the right, in addition to its other rights and remedies, to seek injunctive relief as a remedy or to prevent or curtail any actual or threatened breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations hereunder or for any violation of this Agreement.

Partnership

This Agreement will not create a joint venture, partnership or other formal business relationship or entity of any kind, or an obligation to form any such relationship or entity.  The Receiving Party will act independently and not as an agent of the Disclosing Party for any purpose.

Waiver

The failure of the Disclosing Party at any time to insist on performance of any provision of this Agreement is not a waiver of the Disclosing Party's right at a later time to insist on performance of that or any other governing provision of this Agreement.

Variations and Amendments

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the Parties hereto or their authorized representatives.

Severability

If any paragraph, sub-paragraph, or provision of this Agreement, or the application of such paragraph, sub-paragraph, or provision, is held to be illegal, void, prohibited or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remainder of this Agreement, and the application of the remaining paragraphs, sub-paragraphs, or provisions to persons, or circumstances other than those with respect to which it is held illegal, void, prohibited or unenforceable, shall not be affected.

Governing Law and Jurisdiction

This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales, without reference to its choice of law rules, and shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts at Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

All and any disputes, differences or claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or the construction or the interpretation of any of the clauses hereof or anything done or omitted to be done pursuant thereto, including any question regarding its existence, validity or termination, the Parties shall first endeavour to amicably settle such disputes, differences or claims, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by a sole arbitrator to be jointly appointed by the Parties. The arbitration proceedings shall be held in Bengaluru, India and conducted in English language and such arbitration shall be subject to and governed by the provisions of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 or such other competent law for the time being in force. The cost of the arbitration proceedings shall be borne by the Parties equally. The decision of the sole arbitrator shall be final and binding upon the Parties.

Overriding Effect

This Agreement and any other documents attached hereto or referred herein, integrate all the terms and conditions mentioned herein or incidental hereto, and supersede all oral negotiations and prior writings in respect of the subject matter hereof between the Parties.

Assignment

Neither Party may assign any of its rights or obligations hereunder, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other (not to be unreasonably withheld).

Counterparts:

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which when taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.

Electronic Signature:

Each party agrees that this Agreement and any other documents to be delivered in connection herewith may be electronically signed, and that any electronic signatures appearing on this Agreement, or such other documents are the same as handwritten signatures for the purposes of validity, enforceability, and admissibility.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have entered into this Agreement as of the day and year first above written.

slice small finance bank limited
